description: Queue of section expansion tasks for the analyst. Each item references a curmudgeon review that found major weaknesses. The analyst writes complete replacement text, one item per run.

items.target: KILLSHOT-GAIA section (Part 4 (sections.json part4, formerly part3))

ISS-082/083 mostly already fixed in current text. ISS-074 (archive Live Power page) deferred to poller. Note: EXP-004.json contains SEC-4.8 content from previous run (ISS-418/419); actual SEC-3.1/3.2 expansion is in EXP-004-sec31-32.json.

items.target: Section 6.2 (Eclipse Test section) in data/sections.json part6
items.notes: SEC-6.2 rewritten. Key changes: (1) Dual-baseline structure accurately described (-5 to -26 nT combined). (2) monitor.py computation verified: -18.22 × 0.95 × 1.672 ≈ -28.9 nT (corrected from -29.1). (3) FSF discrepancy documented: monitor.py uses KAPPA (1.672), predictions page uses per-station FSFs (0.642-2.075), inject_ai_layer.py uses latitude_factor — three different formulas. (4) E-PRED tests acknowledged, E-PRED-B flagged as strongest sub-test. (5) Kp qualifier added. (6) W004 provenance and third-attempt pattern included. Note: EXP-005.json contains misrouted EXP-008 taxonomy content; actual SEC-6.2 expansion is in EXP-005-sec62.json.

items.target: WIN-002 detail_evidence in data/wins.json + WIN-048 detail_evidence + detail_verdict_text
items.notes: WIN-002: Complementarity defense already addressed in prior patches; only fix needed is removing stale 'forthcoming section expansion' placeholder sentence. WIN-048: Full rewrite — de-duplicated triple-repeated paragraph, added ΛCDM counter-prediction framing, ecliptic alignment argument, look-elsewhere effect, specific DOI citations (Planck 2015 XXIII, Land & Magueijo 2005), Planck 2018 confirmation. Both kernels of truth acknowledged. Note: EXP-006.json contains old KILLSHOT-GAIA content; actual WIN-002/WIN-048 expansion is in EXP-006-win002-win048.json.

items.target: WIN-024, WIN-031, WIN-032 detail_evidence in data/wins.json
items.notes: WIN-024: Cleaned duplicated paragraphs (longitude mismatch + code evidence each appeared twice), removed duplicated Cnossen 2016 note from claim field, moved coupling research acknowledgment to detail_extra, clarified SAA boundary definition to standard 32,000 nT reference. WIN-031: Expanded from 130 to 280 words — added Eliade axis mundi cross-cultural evidence (Hindu/Norse/Greek/Islamic/Zoroastrian), retrodiction vs prediction distinction, non-discriminating convergence argument, monitor.py absence. WIN-032: Expanded and differentiated from WIN-031 — named category error (future supernatural event as confirmed prediction), added Revelation 21:2 exegesis (pole-axis is dome author's addition), cited geopolitical framing from context.html, quantified scorecard inflation (4 theological WINs), distinguished from WIN-031 (no geometric core).

items.target: Verdict taxonomy review — data/wins.json verdict fields + data/sections.json verdict legend
items.notes: ISS-442: Added subcategory-clarifying paragraph to section 2.6 distinguishing 4 failure modes (data misrepresentation, count inflation, curve-fitting, non-discriminating). ISS-443: Cleaned WIN-043/060 verdict text to remove duplication language, replaced with non-discrimination framing matching Std Model Explains verdict. ISS-445: Added tidal grouping acknowledgment to section 2.3. Also proposed Evaluation Guide addition addressing missing positive verdict category. No verdict reclassifications — taxonomy preserved as 6 categories. Note: EXP-008.json contains old misrouted content; actual taxonomy expansion is in EXP-008-taxonomy.json.

items.target: Section 6.4 (Globe Values Relabeled) — WIN-024 structural inconsistency
items.notes: WIN-024 (Roaring 40s / SAA boundary) is placed in SEC-3.5.4 (relabeling of standard physics) but WIN-024 has relabels_standard=false. The claim is about a spurious correlation (geographic coincidence interpreted as causation), not vocabulary relabeling. The paragraph needs to either be moved to a different section or the section framing needs to distinguish the two error types. Requires prose restructuring of 3.5.4. COMPLETED: Removed WIN-024 paragraph (spurious correlation, not relabeling). Added bridging paragraph naming WIN-024 with cross-reference to detail table. Preserved 3 genuine relabeling examples. Fixed stale SAA latitude range. Output in EXP-009-sec354.json.

items.target: Section 4.8 (Solar Angular Diameter Kill-Shot) in data/sections.json
items.notes: ISS-418: 50% vs 30% numerical inconsistency in our own solar size argument. ISS-419: Missing aetheric refraction defense (dome AI Context Rule 11). Full rewrite needed.
items.integration_note: Analyst confirmed sections.json already contains all required content (ISS-418 50% fix and ISS-419 refraction defense both present). No replacement_html needed.

items.target: Part 4 tab ordering in build-scripts/generate-html.js
items.notes: ISS-453: Tab swap to put Self-Contradictions (selftest) before External Tests (falsify). Key finding: sections.json prose was ALREADY updated to assume this reorder ('above', 'come first') but generate-html.js tab order was never changed — creating a prose/structure inconsistency. Expansion specifies 6 edits to generate-html.js: tab bar buttons, TOC order, 3 sectionNav chain links, div order. No content changes needed. All ~20 showTab() cross-references unaffected (use tab IDs which don't change). Both sides considered: self-contradictions are categorically stronger (dome-parameter-only, no external data to dispute), but Part numbering (4.5 before 4) is a minor visual inconsistency — mitigated by descriptive tab labels.
items.integration_notes: Targets generate-html.js (infrastructure file). Cannot auto-apply. Requires human to apply 6 edits from expansion output. Associated with ISS-453. Decider note: Targets generate-html.js (infrastructure). Cannot auto-apply. See ISS-453 (assigned-analyst). Awaiting human. V6 restructure (commit 6b3e4a0, 2026-04-07) already achieved the tab reordering goal: Self-Contradictions (selftest) is now Part 2, immediately after The Model, before External Tests (falsify = Part 7). ISS-453 was closed as fixed by V6 restructure. EXP-011 formally marked integrated.

items.target: WIN-012 detail_evidence in data/wins.json — spatial heterogeneity loophole + two-eclipse counterargument

ISS-467: spatial heterogeneity closed (three-count: no advance specification, κ not universal if heterogeneous, 7 eclipses with zero replication). ISS-468: two-eclipse rhetoric addressed (BOU=magnetic only, Mohe=gravity only, ratio is retrospective). B(r) 62376/64852 corrected. Bessel vs exponential. OPEN-011 cited. Includes replacement_detail_verdict_text.

items.notes: Full expansion addressing all Cycle 1 + Cycle 2 holes. Bruhn 2006 (4 mathematical errors), Kuhlke 2010, near-field coupling mechanism with λ/2π criterion, precision experiment argument (CUORE+KATRIN+LIGO), dome code analysis (bare metadata + domain 8 mislabeling), theory vs experiment distinction, retracted paper reframed. Dome claim fetched and quoted. Replacement covers detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, and finding fields.

Additional findings: σ discrepancy (9.2σ static vs 14.26σ dynamic), pred=obs identity pattern (3+ domains), NMP 50% adaptive tolerance, 6/20 domains unscored, unfalsifiable thresholds. Kernel of truth acknowledged: infrastructure is genuinely transparent and auditable.

items.target: Eclipse kill-shot section in data/sections.json (part6 or wherever eclipse section lives) — E-PRED-B argument
items.notes: SEC-3.2 eclipse section completely ignores E-PRED-B (Hartland > Ebro differential prediction — dome predicts larger signal at stations with greater coverage reduction, globe does not). This is the dome's strongest pre-registered prediction (geometric/relative, independent of absolute baseline), and our section does not address it at all. Analyst should: (1) describe E-PRED-B formally, (2) explain why it is the dome's strongest test, (3) assess what the current evidence shows about Hartland vs Ebro differential, (4) write replacement prose that acknowledges E-PRED-B and explains why it remains incomplete pending August 2026 eclipse data. — SUPERSEDED: ISS-083 was pre-expansion. E-PRED-B is already thoroughly addressed in data/sections.json part3. No action needed.

items.target: Archive dome Python source files (inject_ai_layer.py, update_optical_caveats.py, monitor.py, recalc_v51.py) from GitHub repo and update Parts 5.14/5.16 claims
items.notes: All claims in Sections 5.14/5.15/5.16 verified against live GitHub repo (john09289/predictions). Eclipse formula confirmed: ecm_eclipse = -18.22 * 0.95 * 1.672 = -28.94 nT. New finding: fsf_derivation.py computes Ebro FSF=1.029 but monitor.py uses 1.672 (63% inflation). check_eclipse_fsf.py confirms author's own sun/firmament collision detection. Two patches provided for sections.json part5. Verbatim archiving deferred until gh CLI available.

items.target: WIN-052 detail_evidence RAR depth + galaxy ontology
items.notes: Full rewrite. Leads with ontology crisis (10^6 scale ratio). Acknowledges kernel of truth (RAR is real). Shows dome has placeholder formula only (ρ_A=g₀ρ_bar/g†, no derivation). MOND priority with transition function and a₀. False dilemma explicitly identified. Cross-WIN structural incoherence in detail_extra covering entire cosmological cluster. Keller & Wadsley + Ludlow citations added. Dome scatter claim corrected (0.057 dex, not zero). 4 anticipated objections pre-closed.

items.target: WIN-053 evidence restructure + V50.6 reframe
items.notes: Full rewrite. Leads with flux conservation derivation (B_south≈39 nT vs fitted 64,852 nT, 1,660:1 self-contradiction). Adds 1/r vs exponential form mismatch (Griffiths §5.3.3). Coefficient provenance critique (fitted to WMM2025 pole strengths, not derived). Debunks dome's false claim that standard dipole predicts zero field at South Pole (~54,000 nT actual). V50.6 reframed: acknowledges improvement, critiques ad hoc motivation. IGRF-13 comparison quantified (200:1 RMS ratio). SAA non-reproducibility. Code search: no B(r) script, no monitoring domain. δ=6,371 km globe fingerprint noted. PRED-TOROID-002 retrodiction flagged (confirmation pre-dates registration).

items.target: WIN-054 El Gordo research + verdict verification
items.notes: CRITICAL FINDING: El Gordo 6.2σ ΛCDM tension is REAL and UNRESOLVED (as of 2025). Our previous text was WRONG — 'modern simulations accommodate El Gordo' is factually incorrect. Kim et al. citation corrected (2021 not 2022, ApJ 923, 101). Asencio et al. 2023 showed tension persists even with revised mass. Molnar & Birkinshaw 2024 confirmed 'extremely unlikely within standard ΛCDM.' Verdict stays 'Not Demonstrated' but reasoning changes: dome correctly identifies genuine tension, but provides no alternative physics (zero equations, zero monitoring, zero cosmological framework). Dome's 'aetheric condensation' has no derivation. Dome's 20,015 km disc has no extragalactic model. Full rewrite of detail_evidence, detail_verdict_text, detail_extra, and finding. 4 anticipated objections pre-closed.

items.target: WIN-058 unified angular coordinates clarification + verdict review
items.notes: COMPLETE + INTEGRATED: Full rewrite based on dome's actual V13 claim (θ = −lonE). Verdict changed to Misleading (bug fix framed as prediction). Previous text attacked V12 strawman (0.9941 scale factor). Integrated in commit b2c73b5.

items.target: Section 4.10 (new) — Cosmological WIN cluster cross-analysis (WIN-054 through WIN-058)
items.notes: Cycle 2 curmudgeon (WIN-054.c2.json) identifies structural pattern: WIN-054 to WIN-058 form a cosmology cluster all new in V51.0. They share the same failure mode (ΛCDM anomalies do not validate dome). A cross-analysis section showing the cluster pattern — and how dome model is exploiting current cosmology tensions without providing dome-specific predictions — would strengthen the argument for all 5 WINs. Should address: (1) why observing a ΛCDM anomaly is not a dome validation, (2) dome has no cosmological model at all (no expansion, no CMB, no large-scale structure), (3) the pattern of cherry-picking open ΛCDM questions as dome wins. VERIFIED: Content already present in data/sections.json part2 (formerly part4b). Section 2.10 Cosmological WIN cluster analysis with V51.0 expansion statistics confirmed in place. Marked complete.

items.target: E-PRED-C paragraph in data/sections.json part4
items.notes: NOTE-004 response: Three-layer gravity incoherence analysis. (1) Dome has no gravitational framework — g(r) formula in monitor.py IS WGS84 with dome labels. (2) SG instruments calibrated via globe physics. (3) E-PRED-C (0.0 μGal) contradicts WIN-012 (κ = 10.9/6.5 — division by zero if gravity signal is null). Van Camp et al. 2001 misrepresented — they concluded null result consistent with standard physics, NOT 'electromagnetic not gravitational.'

items.target: E-PRED-B paragraph in data/sections.json part4
items.notes: NOTE-005 response: FSF tautology proof. FSF = H(r)/r = tan(lat)/tan(ref) by algebraic identity — dome geometry cancels from station-placement equation. Verified 9/9 stations (max error 0.016). fsf_derivation.py in dome repo confirms formula. monitor.py uses hardcoded FSF=1.672. Standard Sq latitude dependence produces same ordering.

items.target: Section 2.9 (V13 Coordinate System) in data/sections.json part2 — e=0.66 eccentricity analysis
items.notes: NOTE-008 response. Three findings: (1) e=0.66 is a secondary globe derivative — sqrt(1-(b/a)²) where b≈15000 fitted to globe geodesics, rounded. (2) E(0.4356)=1.3824 not 1.211 as dome claims — 14.1% mathematical error. (3) No V13 Finsler implementation exists in dome repo — formula never executed in code. Two insertion paragraphs for Section 2.9 after 'parameters are the globe in disguise' paragraph.

items.target: Evidence Exhibits A+B statistical analysis — WIN-011/012 strengthening + dielectric section reinstatement assessment
items.notes: NOTE-009 response. Five deliverables: (1) r=0.992 debunked — expected spurious correlation during G5 storm from shared electromagnetic forcing; Monte Carlo confirms. (2) AI=1.152 is statistically unremarkable — 37% of red-noise samples exceed it. (3) L1B 'mask' claim refuted — standard documented processing (JPL D-22027), no Butterworth filter in actual pipeline. (4) κ circularity: Exhibit A provides no derivation connecting its stats to κ=1.67. (5) Dielectric section reinstatement recommended with broader gravity-coupling scope — flagged for human editorial decision. Code search: r=0.992 and AI=1.152 are hardcoded HTML (same as 95.2% pattern). forensic_unmasking_proof.png returns 404.
items.integration_note: WIN-011 Exhibit A stats (r=0.992, AI=1.152 debunking) added to detail_extra. WIN-012 Exhibit A storm/eclipse contradiction note added to detail_evidence. Dielectric section reinstatement flagged as human editorial decision (scope too large for find/replace patch).

items.target: data/wins.json WIN-002 detail_evidence + detail_extra
items.notes: WIN-002 does not engage the dome's explicit reconciliation argument: "Not contradictory. Complementary. The dome medium damps the resonance (WIN-002), and the damped measured frequency still back-derives the correct dome height (WIN-029)." This argument appears in dome 06-evolution.txt. Our text needs to quote and then rebut this reconciliation. The key rebuttal: (a) back-deriving dome height from an equation that assumes globe-spherical cavity geometry is circular — the damped frequency 7.83 Hz was derived using globe parameters, not dome parameters; (b) WIN-029 uses f = c/(2πa)√2 where a = R_Earth (globe radius), so the "back-derivation" of dome height is actually just the Schumann formula for a globe; (c) invoking aetheric damping as the explanation does not salvage the dome geometry — it just adds an unfalsifiable free parameter. Research: read dome 06-evolution.txt raw-text for exact reconciliation language, then craft response. Cannot fix with find/replace — requires new analytical prose.
items.integration_notes: Decider self-applied: formula-switching arg + Schumann-Tesla r=0.999 debunk added to WIN-002 detail_evidence; Polaris internal inconsistency added to detail_extra; WIN-029 cross-reference appended. Anchor fixed from #win-002 to #win002. 2074 tests pass.

items.target: New section analyzing Steelman Evidence Ladder tiers and killshot.html escape clauses
items.notes: STEELMAN ANALYSIS: The dome built a tiered evidence structure (Evidence Ladder) ranking its claims by strength, plus killshot.html escape clauses. This is the dome's best attempt at self-organization — find the kernel of truth in the tiering, then show why even Tier 1 evidence doesn't discriminate dome from globe. For each tier: acknowledge what's genuinely strong, show why it fails. Killshot: Kp<2 precondition voids test ~50% of the time, three conflicting prediction values (-17 to -21, -28.9, -29.1 nT). This is a new analytical category — may need its own section or integration into existing structure. Analyst decides placement.

Verified content quality before marking complete. Prior analyst run wrote the file but did not update tracker.

items.target: CAT-005 Recommended Proposal Package — V51.1 Refraction Cascade (meta-work, NOT section prose)
items.notes: Human (steve) approved CAT-005 in principle with structural direction: new section under The Model tab (option a). Before fleshing out actual section prose, write a RECOMMENDED PROPOSAL PACKAGE and route through curmudgeon for review. Package must contain: (1) recommended structural placement with rationale (new section under The Model, before individual WIN rebuttals); (2) proposed new code_analysis tag for cascade-affected WINs — propose 2-3 name options with tradeoffs, define semantics rigorously, specify schema impact (boolean field, count placeholder like {{CA_CASCADE_FLIPPED}}); (3) full section outline with per-point sourcing (cite the exact numbers: H(r) ceiling, refraction degrees, n(r) factor, seasonal variation); (4) verdict-flip table — for each of the 12 affected WINs, propose current→new verdict with justification and flag confidence (strong/moderate/weak); retention of data rebuttals as secondary evidence where applicable; (5) documented alternatives (b) per-WIN patches and (c) Section 7.10/7.11 extension — not as scarecrow options but honest tradeoff analysis curmudgeon can weigh; (6) explicit unknowns and risks (e.g. what if curmudgeon rejects the flip for WIN-016 because Gaia parallax is stronger standalone?). Do NOT write actual section prose in this item — that comes after curmudgeon review in a later EXP. Output to monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-050.json with replacement_html: null and a proposal_package field containing the sections above.
items.completion_note: CAT-005 proposal package ready for priority queue routing

items.target: uncounted-failures.json reconciliation — namespace audit (ISS-676)
items.notes: Analyst investigation of ISS-676 uncounted-failures reconciliation concerns. Headline: false alarm — audit conflated W### (weekly) and WIN-### (confirmed) namespaces. No actual ref reuse. Proposes dome_ref_namespace schema upgrade as defensive hedge plus follow-up recommendations to poller (V51.1 raw-text refresh), decider (track silent-failure PENDING weekly slots), and analyst (re-run WIN-058b investigation after poller refresh). See monitor/analyst/expansions/EXP-051.json for full writeup.
items.integration_note: Applied namespace audit schema to uncounted-failures.json: dome_ref_namespace, dome_ref_current, dome_ref_prior, prediction_name fields added; needs_reconciliation cleared for FAIL-001..005 (W###/WIN### namespace confusion was false alarm).

items.target: Part 3 methodology prose — add post_hoc caveat paragraph citing dome author definition + legitimate-novelty carve-out
items.notes: ISS-697: The dome author defines "prospective" as "prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled" (verbatim from john09289.github.io/predictions home page — extracted 2026-04-09: "Prospective = prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled. Not retrofitted. Not explained after the fact. Predicted, then verified."). We apply a stricter scientific standard without addressing his stated one. Task: (1) Write a caveat paragraph for the methodology section (Part 3) that introduces post_hoc reasoning. Must: cite the author's definition verbatim with URL, explain the scientific standard (observation did not exist at prediction time, or predictor provably had no knowledge of it, with timestamped pre-registration), define the legitimate-novelty carve-out (unique framework + unknown-to-author data = legitimate independent discovery), and define the trivial-transcription test (value in physics textbook + no pre-registration = not a prediction). ALSO: Note the smoking-gun evidence — dome monitor scripts typically hardcode expected values in the same commit as the live fetch, making even the author's weaker self-definition unverifiable. DATA DEPENDENCY CONFIRMED: home-page quote fetched 2026-04-09 from john09289.github.io/predictions — see also monitor/analyst/human-notes.json ISS-697.tasks[1]. Cross-reference EXP-050/CAT-005 for consistent language on verdict criteria.
items.data_dependency_note: Verbatim quote retrieved 2026-04-09: "Prospective = prediction was timestamped before confirming data was pulled. Not retrofitted. Not explained after the fact. Predicted, then verified." URL: https://john09289.github.io/predictions

items.target: Per-WIN post_hoc audit — all WINs tagged post_hoc:true, add rebuttal sentence citing author definition
items.notes: ISS-697: Audit every WIN currently tagged post_hoc: true. For each: (1) Check whether existing rebuttal text addresses the author's definition ("timestamped before confirming data was pulled") or merely asserts post-hoc. Where it does not, add a sentence citing the author's definition and explaining why this specific value fails the novelty or pre-registration test. (2) Classify the value itself: common-knowledge (textbook), field-specific published, or obscure. Common-knowledge cases should note that explicitly. Obscure cases may actually strengthen the author's case — flag for verdict review. (3) Check dome repo git history for genuine pre-registration: are there commits where the expected value was added BEFORE the fetch call? (Given inject_ai_layer.py patterns, expected: no clean pre-registration.) (4) Cross-reference ISS-677 (Prospective Wins Mode 5) — criteria must use same standard. DO NOT start writing prose before EXP-053 methodology paragraph is complete and curmudgeon-reviewed — use consistent language. BLOCKED ON: EXP-053 completion (methodology caveat defines the language). Also cross-reference EXP-050/CAT-005 for consistent verdict criteria language.

items.target: WIN-067 citation verification: verify DOI 10.1029/2019JB018855 resolves to Ebbing et al. 2021 JGR paper about Antarctic mantle. AGU DOIs encode submission year (2019 submission, 2021 publication is normal for AGU). If publication year is not 2021, update citation.
items.notes: Fetch the DOI URL and confirm: (a) paper title mentions East Antarctic mantle/gravity/cratons, (b) publication year is 2021, (c) Ebbing is an author. If all correct, close as wontfix (AGU submission-year DOI encoding is expected). If wrong year, patch citation.
items.completion_note: Analyst confirmed DOI wrong paper. Correct: 10.1029/2019JB017997 (Pappa et al. 2019). Two find/replace patches ready.
